description: The ACT Wellbeing Framework looks at 12 aspects of wellbeing – which we call ‘domains’. columns.name: DataStatus
columns.description: Five possible values - “Live”, “InsufficientLive”, “LiveMissing”, “HistoryMissing” and “ReplacedLiveDifferent”. See below for explanation of each status; “Live” - all data used to generate the prediction is based on live data “InsufficientLive” - Not all data used to generate the prediction is based on live data. Where live data is available, but not considered statistically valid on a segment along the route, it is replaced with historical values for the segment. “LiveMissing” - Predictions cannot be made as there is no live data available for at least one segment. No route predictions can be made. Equivalent to an error status. “HistoryMissing” - Predictions cannot be made as there is no historical data available for at least one segment. Historical data is needed to predict changes in travel times for downstream segments. No route predictions can be made. Equivalent to an error status. “ReplacedLiveDifferent” - Similar to the “InsufficientLive” status, except this is a warning to state that the historical data replacing the live data has resulted in a prediction that may be more than 5 minutes less than actual travel times.

GVG - A = EPS 16 or more, GVG - B = EPS 14 or more but less than 16, GVG - C = EPS 9.5 or more but less than 14, GVG - D = EPS less than 9.5. CO2 Emissions Ratings are based on CO2 emissions. CO2 - AAA = 0g per kilometre, CO2 - AA = 1g to 65g per kilometre, CO2 - A = 66g to 130g per kilometre, CO2 - B = 131g to 175g per kilometre, CO2 - C = 176g to 220g per kilometre, CO2 - D = more than 220g per kilometre

description: In the ACT, native wildlife are protected under the Nature Conservation Act 2014 (the NC Act). Greater protection for both native plants and animals is provided through listing processes.
